food_name: Koka Instant Noodles Mushroom Flavour (1 packet, 85g, prepared)
aliases: 可口蘑菇面 (Kě Kǒu Mó Gū Miàn)
கோகா நூடுல்ஸ் காளான் சுவை (Kōkā Nūṭuls Kāḷāṉ Cuvai)

chinese: 可口蘑菇面 (Kě Kǒu Mó Gū Miàn)
tamil: கோகா நூடுல்ஸ் காளான் சுவை (Kōkā Nūṭuls Kāḷāṉ Cuvai)

food_subgroup: Instant noodles — soup-style, fried noodle block with flavour sachet, vegetarian mushroom flavour

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for branded Koka Mushroom Flavour instant noodles. FOODON_03315595 (instant noodle food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A039V covers instant noodles as a category; no brand-specific code exists for Koka Mushroom Flavour.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for branded instant noodle products.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one full packet (85g dry weight), prepared as soup noodles by dissolving the flavour sachet in boiling water with the cooked noodle block.

nutrient_source.title: Koka Instant Noodles Mushroom Flavour — Nutrition Information Panel, Tat Hui Foods Singapore product label
nutrient_source.organization: Tat Hui Foods Pte Ltd — Singapore product Nutrition Information Panel (NIP)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Koka Mushroom Flavour product Nutrition Information Panel as declared by Tat Hui Foods Pte Ltd, Singapore. Values are per 100g of prepared product. This is an approved source under the USDA Branded Food / manufacturer NIP category for packaged commercial products.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — instant noodles analogous entry
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of ~47 (low) is referenced from the University of Sydney GI Database for instant noodles as a category. Koka Mushroom Flavour has not been independently tested; the value is an analogous estimate based on wheat-flour fried instant noodle products of comparable composition. Glycemic load per 85g serving calculated as: (47 × 56.0g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 ≈ 26.3. Available carbohydrate per serving = 65.9 × 0.85 − dietary fibre contribution ≈ 56.0g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — 882mg per 100g; approximately 750mg per 85g packet (37.5% of the 2,000mg daily limit)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit or avoid consuming the soup broth; discarding the broth reduces sodium intake from this meal by approximately 60–70%, bringing sodium from the noodle block alone to an estimated 225–300mg per packet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~47, low GI category) but high glycemic load (~26 per packet) due to large carbohydrate portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low GI of ~47 is favourable compared to rice or bread, but the glycemic load of ~26 per full packet is significant; patients with Type 2 Diabetes should consider consuming a half-packet portion or pairing with non-starchy vegetables and a protein source to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4.7g per 100g (approximately 4.0g per packet) from palm oil used in the frying process; zero trans fat confirmed on label
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should note the saturated fat contribution from palm oil in the fried noodle block; trans fat is confirmed zero per the product label, which is a relative advantage over older instant noodle formulations, but overall saturated fat intake should be monitored in the context of total daily dietary fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Vegetarian formulation with no processed meat additives; lower saturated fat than most competing instant noodle brands
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The vegetarian mushroom flavour formulation avoids processed meat additives that may be pro-inflammatory; the mushroom flavouring may contain trace ergosterol (a Vitamin D2 precursor) from dried mushroom extract, though the amount is clinically trivial.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Koka Instant Noodles Mushroom Flavour (可口蘑菇面) is a Singapore-manufactured instant noodle product produced by Tat Hui Foods Pte Ltd at their Tuas facility. The brand name 'KOKA' is derived from 'kokatsu', a Japanese word meaning vigorous, and has been a source of local pride since the company's founding in 1986. Each 85g packet contains a deep-fried wheat noodle block and a mushroom-flavoured seasoning sachet. The noodles are prepared by boiling the block in water and dissolving the sachet to form a savoury soup broth. The texture is soft and springy, with a mild umami mushroom flavour that is lighter and less pungent than Korean or Japanese instant noodle competitors. Koka Mushroom Flavour is one of the few mainstream Singapore instant noodle products that is explicitly vegetarian and vegan — using no meat-based seasonings — and carries halal certification. It is popular in Singapore school canteens, army camps, and budget households. Nutritionally, the product provides 424 kcal per 100g (approximately 360 kcal per packet), with 9.4g protein, 12.9g fat (4.7g saturated from palm oil, zero trans fat), and 65.9g carbohydrate per 100g. Sodium is 882mg per 100g (approximately 750mg per packet), which is lower than many competing brands such as Shin Ramyun or Nissin Cup Noodles, but still represents approximately 37.5% of the recommended 2,000mg daily sodium limit. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 47 (low GI), though the glycemic load per full packet is approximately 26, which is clinically significant for patients managing blood glucose. Discarding the soup broth after cooking reduces sodium intake by an estimated 60–70%. The brand also produces a 'Purple Wheat' variant with added purple wheat flour that carries the Singapore HPB Healthier Choice Symbol.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Koka Mushroom Flavour product Nutrition Information Panel as declared by Tat Hui Foods Pte Ltd on the Singapore product label. This is an exact match to the branded product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for instant noodles, which reduces GI confidence slightly, but all other data points are manufacturer-verified.
notes: 1. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per Tat Hui Foods product NIP label declaration. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per product label; this is consistent with a vegetarian, non-egg, non-dairy formulation. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_b12_mcg are 0.0 — confirmed zero or not present per product label; these are expected for a wheat-flour instant noodle product with no fortification of these nutrients declared. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0: detailed fatty acid breakdown is not reported on the Koka Mushroom Flavour product NIP label and is not available in the Singapore HPB database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific branded product as of April 2026. water_g is set to -1.0: moisture content for the prepared product is not reported on the product label and is not available in approved sources for this specific SKU. 3. GI value of 47 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for instant noodles as a category; Koka Mushroom Flavour has not been independently GI-tested. Glycemic load per 85g serving is approximately 26.3. 4. Sodium per 85g packet is approximately 750mg (882mg per 100g × 0.85); discarding the soup broth reduces sodium by an estimated 60–70%. 5. FoodOn URI FOODON_03315595 and FoodEx2 code A039V are closest-parent matches; no brand-specific ontology codes exist for this product. 6. The product is manufactured by Tat Hui Foods Pte Ltd, Tuas, Singapore, and is exported to over 50 countries. The brand name derives from 'kokatsu' (Japanese: vigorous). 7.
